Implementation of an SSH2 "message".

d.d/

Z d:d;
Z!d<d=
Messagea
    An SSH2 message is a stream of bytes that encodes some combination of
    strings, integers, bools, and infinite-precision integers (known in Python
    as longs).  This class builds or breaks down such a byte stream.
    Normally you don't need to deal with anything this low-level, but it's
    exposed for people implementing custom extensions, or features that
    paramiko doesn't support yet.
    l
        Create a new SSH2 message.
        :param str content:
            the byte stream to use as the message content (passed in only when
            decomposing a message).
        N)
packet)
selfZ
content

__init__+
Message.__init__c
        Return the byte stream content of this message, as a string/bytes obj.
        )

Message.__str__c
        Returns a string representation of this object, for debugging.
        z
paramiko.Message(
reprr
getvaluer

Message.__repr__c
        Return the byte stream content of this Message, as bytes.
        )
Message.asbytesc
        Rewind the message to the beginning as if no items had been parsed
        out of it yet.
        r
seekr
rewindJ
Message.rewindc
        Return the bytes (as a `str`) of this message that haven't already been
        parsed and returned.
        )
tell
readr
positionZ	remainderr
get_remainderQ
Message.get_remainderc
        Returns the `str` bytes of this message that have been parsed and
        returned. The string passed into a message's constructor can be
        regenerated by concatenating ``get_so_far`` and `get_remainder`.
        )
get_so_far[
Message.get_so_farc
        Return the next ``n`` bytes of the message (as a `str`), without
        decomposing into an int, decoded string, etc.  Just the raw bytes are
        returned. Returns a string of ``n`` zero bytes if there weren't ``n``
        bytes remaining in the message.
        i
lenr
max_pad_sizer
	get_bytese
Message.get_bytesc
        Return the next byte of the message, without decomposing it.  This
        is equivalent to `get_bytes(1) <get_bytes>`.
        :return:
            the next (`str`) byte of the message, or ``'
'`` if there aren't
            any bytes remaining.
        
get_byter
Message.get_bytec
        Fetch a boolean from the stream.
        r"
get_boolean}
Message.get_booleanc
        Fetch an int from the stream.
        :return: a 32-bit unsigned `int`.
        r"
inflate_long
get_binary
struct
unpack)
byter
get_adaptive_int
Message.get_adaptive_intc
        Fetch an int from the stream.
        r'
get_int
Message.get_intc
        Fetch a 64-bit int from the stream.
        :return: a 64-bit unsigned integer (`long`).
        
	get_int64
Message.get_int64c
        Fetch a long int (mpint) from the stream.
        :return: an arbitrary-length integer (`long`).
        )
	get_mpint
Message.get_mpintc
        Fetch a `str` from the stream.  This could be a byte string and may
        contain unprintable characters.  (It's not unheard of for a string to
        contain another byte-stream message.)
        
get_string
Message.get_stringc
        Fetch a Unicode string from the stream.
        )
get_text
Message.get_textc
        Fetch a string from the stream.  This could be a byte string and may
        contain unprintable characters.  (It's not unheard of for a string to
        contain another byte-stream Message.)
        r4
Message.get_binaryc
        Fetch a list of `strings <str>` from the stream.
        These are trivially encoded as comma-separated values in a string.
        
splitr
get_list
Message.get_listc
        Write bytes to the stream, without any formatting.
        :param str b: bytes to add
        
writer$
	add_bytes
Message.add_bytesc
        Write a single byte to the stream, without any formatting.
        :param str b: byte to add
        r:
add_byte
Message.add_bytec
        Add a boolean value to the stream.
        :param bool b: boolean value to add
        )
add_boolean
Message.add_booleanc
        Add an integer to the stream.
        :param int n: integer to add
        r'
pack
add_int
Message.add_intc
big_intr
add_stringr
deflate_longr*
add_adaptive_int
Message.add_adaptive_intc
        Add a 64-bit int to the stream.
        :param long n: long int to add
        r0
	add_int64
Message.add_int64c
        Add a long int to the stream, encoded as an infinite-precision
        integer.  This method only works on positive numbers.
        :param long z: long int to add
        )
	add_mpint
Message.add_mpintc
        Add a string to the stream.
        :param str s: string to add
        )
Message.add_stringc
        Add a list of strings to the stream.  They are encoded identically to
        a single string of values separated by commas.  (Yes, really, that's
        how SSH2 does it.)
        :param l: list of strings to add
        r7
join)
add_list
Message.add_listc
type
boolr>
isinstancer
listrN
_add!
Message._addc
        Add a sequence of items to the stream.  The values are encoded based
        on their type: str, int, bool, list, or long.
        .. warning::
            Longs are encoded non-deterministically.  Don't use this method.
        :param seq: the sequence of items
        N)
itemr
add+
Message.add)
